no R code, no API, no math content change).

**PR / branch**: this PR / `agent/notation-switch-ns3b-r-roxygen`.

**Lane**: Claude (Codex absent).

**Dispatched by**: maintainer 2026-05-14 "let's go Psi!" +
Option 2 split confirmation. NS-3b is the cosmetic roxygen
half of NS-3 (NS-3a was the atomic API rename).

**Files touched** (~22 files):

R/ roxygen prose (8 files):
- `R/extract-sigma.R` (6 `\eqn{}` sites)
- `R/extract-omega.R` (6 sites: 2 in `\eqn{}`, 4 in comments)
- `R/unique-keyword.R` (4 sites in `\eqn{}` + 1 in `@examples` comment)
- `R/extractors.R` (5 sites in `\eqn{}`)
- `R/fit-multi.R` (5 sites in internal comments)
- `R/extract-two-U-cross-check.R` (3 sites: 1 in `\eqn{}`, 2 in comments)
- `R/extract-two-U-via-PIC.R` (4 sites in mixed contexts)
- `R/brms-sugar.R` (5 sites)

man/*.Rd regenerated by `devtools::document()` (14 files):
- `man/latent.Rd`, `man/phylo_unique.Rd`, `man/indep.Rd`,
  `man/dep.Rd`, `man/extract_residual_split.Rd`,
  `man/extract_Omega.Rd`, `man/extract_Sigma.Rd`,
  `man/extract_two_U_via_PIC.Rd`,
  `man/compare_PIC_vs_joint.Rd`, `man/extract_Sigma_B.Rd`,

  `man/extract_communality.Rd`, `man/diag_re.Rd`.

dev-log: this after-task file.

## Math contract

No R code, no API, no math content change. Only the symbol
used in math prose. `\mathbf{S}` -> `\boldsymbol{\Psi}`,
`\mathbf S` -> `\boldsymbol\Psi`, `S_B/S_W/S_phy/S_non` in
plain comments -> `Psi_B/Psi_W/Psi_phy/Psi_non`, `s_phy/s_non`
(lowercase) -> `psi_phy/psi_non`. Matches the convention
established in NS-1 (decisions.md 2026-05-14 entry).

## Substitution rules (perl one-pass)

```perl
# LaTeX math forms
s/\\mathbf\{S\}_B/\\boldsymbol{\\Psi}_B/g;
s/\\mathbf\{S\}_W/\\boldsymbol{\\Psi}_W/g;
s/\\mathbf S_B\b/\\boldsymbol\\Psi_B/g;
s/\\mathbf S_W\b/\\boldsymbol\\Psi_W/g;
s/\\mathbf\{S\}/\\boldsymbol{\\Psi}/g;
s/\\mathbf S\b/\\boldsymbol\\Psi/g;
# Plain prose tokens
s/\bS_phy\b/Psi_phy/g;
s/\bS_non\b/Psi_non/g;
s/\bs_phy\b/psi_phy/g;
s/\bs_non\b/psi_non/g;
# Bare S_B / S_W in inline math
s/(\\Lambda_B\^[^+]+\+ )S_B\b/$1\\boldsymbol\\Psi_B/g;
s/(\\Lambda_W\^[^+]+\+ )S_W\b/$1\\boldsymbol\\Psi_W/g;
# Plain S_B / S_W
s/\bS_B\b/Psi_B/g;
s/\bS_W\b/Psi_W/g;
# diag(s)
s/diag\(s\)/diag(psi)/g;
```

## Checks run

- `rg -n '\\bS_B\\b|\\bS_W\\b|\\bS_phy\\b|\\bS_non\\b|\\\\mathbf\{?S\}?_|\\\\mathbf S\\b' R/extract-sigma.R ... R/brms-sugar.R`
  returned **zero hits** after the perl pass.
- `devtools::document(quiet = TRUE)`: regenerated 14 `man/*.Rd`
  files cleanly. Post-doc protocol (PR #36 lesson):
  `tail -5 man/extract_Sigma.Rd` reads cleanly;
  `grep -c '^\\keyword' man/extract_Sigma.Rd` = 0. Same for
  `man/extract_Omega.Rd`.
- No `devtools::test()` run for this PR: NS-3b is roxygen-
  prose-only; no R code or test fixture touched. Tests on
  NS-3a's branch (PR #88) already validated the engine + API
  rename.

## Consistency audit

- All `\mathbf{S}` / `\mathbf S` replaced by
  `\boldsymbol{\Psi}` / `\boldsymbol\Psi`.
- All `S_phy`, `S_non`, `s_phy`, `s_non` in plain prose
  replaced.
- `S_B` and `S_W` in inline math `\eqn{}` blocks replaced
  with `\boldsymbol\Psi_B` / `\boldsymbol\Psi_W`.
- `S_B` and `S_W` in plain comments replaced with `Psi_B` /
  `Psi_W`.

Function- and file-name "two-U" task labels preserved per
`decisions.md` 2026-05-14 entry (math = canonical
`\boldsymbol\Psi`; function names = legacy task labels).

## Tests of the tests

No tests in this PR. The roxygen prose change is invisible
to the runtime. If pkgdown / `R CMD check` cared about the
math LaTeX, the `pkgdown::check_pkgdown()` run scheduled for
NS-5 would catch a malformed `\eqn{}` block. So far the
sweep is mechanical and the regex preserves brace structure.

## What went well

- The 8 R/ files were a clean perl substitution: zero
  context-dependent edits, no special cases. The LaTeX
  forms `\mathbf{S}` (braced) vs `\mathbf S` (unbraced) both
  picked up by separate substitutions; the plain-prose
  `S_phy` / `S_non` tokens picked up by word-boundary regex.
- `devtools::document()` regenerated 14 `man/*.Rd` files
  cleanly. Post-doc spot-check (PR #36 lesson protocol)
  shows no malformed `\keyword` entries.
- Combined with NS-3a (which is still in CI as PR #88), the
  notation switch reaches into every R/ file. The remaining
  scope is articles (NS-4 + NS-5).

## What did not go smoothly

- Perl regex initially nearly mangled the `\Lambda_B^\top +
  S_B` context (`S_B` appears bare in some `\eqn{}` blocks
  after a `\Lambda^\top +` term). Caught by adding an
  explicit `(\\Lambda_B\^[^+]+\+ )S_B\b` substitution
  *before* the bare-`S_B` rule. The order of perl
  substitutions matters; first sweep handled the
  context-bearing form, then the catch-all.
- Some plain-prose contexts use `Psi_B` (no LaTeX) and
  others use `\boldsymbol\Psi_B` (LaTeX). The substitution
  picked the right form per surrounding markup, but reading
  the diff requires attention to whether the line is inside
  `\eqn{}` or not.

## Team learning, per AGENTS.md role

- **Ada (maintainer)**: the perl-one-pass model continues to
  work well for low-ambiguity bulk renames. NS-3a + NS-3b
  together touch ~50 R/ + test + article files; no manual
  edit per file was required.
- **Boole (R API)**: no API change in NS-3b. The roxygen
  `@param` / `@return` / `@examples` for any user-facing
  function with `S_*` was already handled in NS-3a (where
  argument-name renames lived).
- **Gauss (TMB likelihood / numerical)**: NS-3b is roxygen-
  prose only; no engine-level change. The internal comments
  in `R/fit-multi.R` referencing `S_phy` / `s_phy` are
  pedagogical only. The TMB template internal variable names
  (in `src/gllvmTMB.cpp`) are private to the implementation
  and unchanged.
- **Noether (math consistency)**: the math is consistent
  per the NS-1 convention. The internal-comment
  `Sigma_phy = Lambda_phy Lambda_phy^T + Psi_phy` form now
  matches the article body (after NS-4/5) and the design
  doc 03-phylogenetic-gllvm.md (after NS-2).
- **Darwin (biology audience)**: no audience-facing change.
- **Fisher (statistical inference)**: no inference-machinery
  change. Standing brief for Phase 1b' (Profile-CI
  validation milestone with galamm Wald-only comparator).
- **Emmy (R package architecture)**: `man/*.Rd` regenerated
  cleanly; reference index will reflect the new
  `\boldsymbol\Psi` notation after pkgdown re-renders.
- **Pat (applied PhD user)**: standing brief for Phase 1c
  article ports; NS-3b doesn't change the user-facing
  surface.
- **Jason (literature scout)**: standing brief for the
  pre-Phase-1b' galamm landscape scan (already done in this
  session; results recorded in plan file).
- **Curie (simulation / testing)**: no test fixtures touched.
- **Grace (CI / pkgdown / CRAN)**: pkgdown re-render
  scheduled for NS-5; will produce the final user-visible
  reference index with `\boldsymbol\Psi` everywhere.
- **Rose (systems audit)**: pre-publish audit confirms the
  perl sweep was complete (zero remaining hits in NS-3b
  scope). Combined with NS-3a (still pending merge as
  PR #88), R/ and tests are now fully on the new convention.
- **Shannon (cross-team)**: Codex absent.

## Design-doc + pkgdown updates

- No design-doc edits in NS-3b (design docs handled in
  NS-2).
- `pkgdown::check_pkgdown()` not re-run in this PR;
  scheduled for NS-5.

## Known limitations and next actions

**Known limitations**:

- Articles (11 files) still use `\mathbf{S}_B` / `\mathbf S_X`
  / `S_phy` etc. in math prose. NS-4 and NS-5 close this.
- The `\boldsymbol\Psi_B` form in roxygen vs `\boldsymbol{S}_B`
  in functional-biogeography.Rmd (still on PR #82 / Batch C
  state) is a temporary inconsistency that NS-5 resolves.

**Next actions**:

1. NS-4: articles part 1 math prose sweep -- Concepts +
   lighter Worked examples. Mechanical perl sweep similar
   to NS-3b.
2. NS-5: articles part 2 math prose sweep -- heavier Worked
   examples (`behavioural-syndromes.Rmd`,
   `functional-biogeography.Rmd`, `phylogenetic-gllvm.Rmd`) +
   `NEWS.md` entry explaining the reversal + final
   `pkgdown::check_pkgdown()` sanity check.
3. After NS-1 through NS-5 merged + pkgdown re-renders:
   start Phase 1a Batch A under the new convention.
